evals.prompt: Are our pages competing against each other for the same searches?
evals.expected_output: Should use report id cannibalisation to find search queries that appear across several URLs and decide whether the overlap needs attention. Should follow the discovery flow: seo_list_reports with category opportunities, seo_describe_report with id cannibalisation, then seo_run_report with the required site. Should read dataStatus and both source.pageExposure and source.propertyDemand with their validation counts and completeness first. Should clarify URL overlap is a review candidate, not proven harmful cannibalisation.
assertions: Uses report id cannibalisation for the query and URL overlap review
Follows seo_list_reports, seo_describe_report, seo_run_report in order
Reads dataStatus and both source.pageExposure and source.propertyDemand first
Reads the validation counts and completeness for each source
States URL overlap is a review candidate, not proven harmful cannibalisation

evals.prompt: Run the overlap report for sc-domain:example.com over 90 days, top 30 queries, at least 100 impressions.
evals.expected_output: Should run report id cannibalisation with site sc-domain:example.com, days 90, limit 30, and minImpressions 100. Should read selection to see why groups were excluded or suppressed, then per-page clicks, impressions, position, impression share, reviewContext, and HHI, then priority.score and suggestedOwnerUrl. Should use only parameters defined in the schema.
assertions: Runs report id cannibalisation with days 90, limit 30, and minImpressions 100
Reads selection to see why groups were excluded or suppressed
Reads per-page clicks, impressions, position, impression share, reviewContext, and HHI
Reads priority.score and suggestedOwnerUrl
Uses only parameters defined in the report schema

evals.prompt: Just redirect every overlapping URL into the suggestedOwnerUrl to fix the cannibalisation.
evals.expected_output: Should decline to consolidate automatically on the mechanical pick. Should state suggestedOwnerUrl is a low-confidence mechanical pick that requires intent review, and that URL overlap is a review candidate, not proven harmful cannibalisation, so not every multi-URL query is a problem. Should recommend comparing intent and live SERPs for the overlapping URLs before consolidating anything.
assertions: Declines to redirect every overlapping URL automatically
States suggestedOwnerUrl is a low-confidence mechanical pick that requires intent review
States URL overlap is a review candidate, not proven harmful cannibalisation
Notes not every multi-URL query is a problem
Recommends comparing intent and live SERPs before consolidating

evals.prompt: priority.score is the click lift from merging, and the empty result means we have no cannibalisation anywhere.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse both readings. Should state priority.score is a heuristic, not estimated click lift, and that a partial source cannot support an all-clear. Should read the validation counts and completeness on source.pageExposure and source.propertyDemand and the selection suppressions before concluding, keeping partial, suppressed, and complete states distinct.
assertions: Refuses to read priority.score as estimated click lift
States priority.score is a heuristic
States a partial source cannot support an all-clear
Reads validation counts and completeness and selection suppressions before concluding
Keeps partial, suppressed, and complete states distinct
